q: Bernoulli's equation, in fluid dynamics, relates the pressure, flow speed, and height for the steady (laminar) flow of an ideal ___ fluid.
For an ideal incompressible fluid in Bernoulli's equation, the internal friction of the fluid flow is ___.
Bernoulli's equation was first derived in 1738 by the Swiss mathematician Daniel ___.
Bernoulli's equation can be derived using the ___ principle to a fluid flowing in a tube.
Bernoulli's theorem states that the sum of pressure energy, kinetic energy and potential energy per unit volume of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains ___.
According to the work-energy principle, the work done on a section of fluid must equal the change in total ___ energy.
Total mechanical energy is the sum of kinetic energy and ___ energy.
Since the fluid is incompressible, $A_1 \Delta x_1 = A_2 \Delta x_2 = \text{___}$.
The work done on the fluid due to pressure is $W = (P_1 - \text{___})\Delta V$.
The change in kinetic energy is $\Delta KE = \frac{1}{2}\rho \Delta V (v_2^2 - \text{___})$.
The change in potential energy is $\Delta PE = \rho \Delta V g (h_2 - \text{___})$.
